youtube_items.title: [YT] 2025 UNESCO Confucius Prize for Literacy highlights global education efforts - YouTube
youtube_items.summary: A celebration of both heritage and humanity, the 20th UNESCO Confucius Prize for Literacy ceremony took place in Shufu, hometown of the Asian Chinese philosopher. These years awards recognize three projects from Bangladesh, Ireland and Morocco for their outstanding contributions to inclusive education. We believe that people should have the rights to have their literacy needs mesh, and by having this prize, it gives international recognition for that work, but also will help us to fund and sustain the work in the years to come. It gives up a lot of strength. Hopefully, we will be able to scale up more communities and internationally in other countries. analysis.problem: Global math gender gap is widening, with girls' math gains slowing down, despite increased investment in preschool programs and data-driven approaches to education.
analysis.root_cause: The widening gender gap in math education is driven by systemic inequities in early childhood education, lack of targeted support for girls, and the persistence of stereotypes that limit female participation in STEM fields.
leverage_points: Education systems and policymakers are the primary actors in addressing this issue.
Data-driven approaches and early intervention programs are critical to closing the gap.

proposed_actions.action: Implement targeted early childhood education programs for girls, with a focus on math and STEM skills.
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase girls' math proficiency by 15% in preschool programs within 2 years.

proposed_actions.action: Develop and scale multi-lingual literacy and math education tools for under-resourced communities.
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Expand access to digital learning resources in low-income regions by 2028.

evidence: Global math gender gap report: 'Whatever we do, the action we take to address the issue must start quite early and must be very targeted.'
Preschool enrollment and spending are at record highs, but program quality remains a concern.
analysis.generalization: This issue is interconnected with education quality, data-driven decision-making, and systemic inequities in early childhood education. It affects not only girls but also the broader development of future leaders and innovators.
analysis.summary: The widening math gender gap highlights the need for early intervention and targeted education programs to ensure equitable learning opportunities for all students. Addressing this issue is critical for building a more inclusive and equitable education system.

raw_items.title: Literacy
raw_items.summary: Literacy is the ability to read and write, and illiteracy is the inability to read and write. Some researchers suggest that the study of literacy as a concept can be divided into two periods: the period before 1950, when literacy was understood solely as alphabetical literacy (understanding the meanings of words without necessarily being able to use words); and the period after 1950, when literacy

raw_items.title: What you need to know about literacy
raw_items.summary: March 24, 2026 - Literacy is a continuum of learning and proficiency in reading, writing and using numbers throughout life and is part of a larger set of skills, which include digital skills, media literacy, education for sustainable development and global citizenship as well as job-specific skills.
